frontier_justification: Fault-tolerant quantum computing via surface code error correction is at a pivotal moment in 2025-2026. Google Willow (2024) demonstrated below-threshold error suppression, making this one of the most closely watched technology developments in fundamental physics.

papers.title: Surface codes: Towards practical large-scale quantum computation
papers.key_contribution: Comprehensive surface code blueprint: threshold analysis, resource overhead estimation, and full fault-tolerant gate set for planar qubit array topology.

idea_genome.problem_genome: Quantum systems decohere; need practical error correction scheme compatible with nearest-neighbor 2D qubit connectivity.
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: Planar surface code: data qubits on lattice, syndrome qubits on edges; X and Z stabilizer measurements; logical qubit encoded in code distance d array.
idea_genome.observation_genome: Surface code threshold ~1%; 10^9 logical operations require ~10^4 physical qubits per logical qubit at 0.1% physical error rate.
idea_genome.limitation_genome: Enormous qubit overhead (10^4:1 ratio); requires fast syndrome extraction; theory far ahead of experimental hardware capability.

papers.title: Superconducting quantum circuits at the surface code threshold for fault tolerance
papers.key_contribution: Demonstrates two-qubit gate fidelity of 99.4% in superconducting qubits, meeting the surface code threshold for the first time experimentally.

idea_genome.problem_genome: Surface code theory requires <1% physical error rates; superconducting qubits historically far above this threshold.
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: Improved transmon qubit design; optimized pulse sequences; cross-resonance two-qubit gates; fidelity benchmarking via randomized benchmarking.
idea_genome.observation_genome: Two-qubit gate error of 0.6% (99.4% fidelity) demonstrated; single-qubit gates at 99.9%; first experimental surface code threshold crossing.
idea_genome.limitation_genome: Demonstrated on 5 qubits only; syndrome extraction not yet demonstrated; 2D array with full error correction cycle not yet implemented.

papers.title: Exponential suppression of bit or phase errors with cyclic error correction
papers.key_contribution: 1D repetition code on 21 qubits shows exponential error suppression with code distance; first demonstration of logical error rate decreasing with qubit number.

idea_genome.problem_genome: Threshold crossing in 2-qubit gates does not guarantee that error suppression improves with code size in practice.
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: 21-qubit linear chain; cyclic repetition code (distance 5,7,11); repeated syndrome extraction cycles; correlated error decoding.
idea_genome.observation_genome: Logical error rate decreases exponentially with code distance; repetition code logical error rate 100x better than physical qubits; but only 1D.
idea_genome.limitation_genome: 1D repetition code cannot correct both X and Z errors; not a full surface code; 2D full QEC not yet demonstrated.

papers.title: Suppressing quantum errors by scaling a surface code logical qubit
papers.key_contribution: 2D surface code on 72-qubit Sycamore chip: distance-5 outperforms distance-3 logical qubit; below-threshold operation for one QEC cycle type.

idea_genome.problem_genome: 1D repetition code (2021) is insufficient; need 2D surface code demonstration showing quality improves with scale.
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: 72-qubit Sycamore; distance-3 (17 qubits) vs distance-5 (49 qubits) surface codes; repeated QEC cycles; minimum-weight perfect matching decoder.
idea_genome.observation_genome: Distance-5 logical qubit outperforms distance-3; first 2D surface code scaling demonstration; but not yet clearly below threshold for full code.
idea_genome.limitation_genome: Partial below-threshold: only for one error type clearly; full fault-tolerant computation not yet demonstrated; decoder latency remains limiting factor.

papers.title: Quantum error correction below the surface code threshold
papers.key_contribution: Willow chip: 105-qubit superconducting processor demonstrates below-threshold surface code error correction with exponential suppression across d=3,5,7; logical error rates 2× per distance level.

idea_genome.problem_genome: 2023 paper showed scaling works partially; need clear demonstration that both X and Z error rates decrease exponentially with code distance.
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: 105-qubit 'Willow' processor; improved qubit coherence (average T1~68μs); optimized readout; rapid classical decoding (1μs per round).
idea_genome.observation_genome: Every step in code distance (d=3→5→7) halves logical error rate; d=7 logical qubit 10× better than best physical qubit; classical simulation exponentially hard.
idea_genome.limitation_genome: Only QEC demonstrated, not full fault-tolerant computation; magic state distillation and logical gate operations not yet demonstrated.

papers.title: Fault-Tolerant Execution of Error-Corrected Quantum Algorithms
papers.key_contribution: First demonstration of end-to-end fault-tolerant execution of actual quantum algorithms (QAOA and HHL) on logical qubits; 12 logical qubits on 97 physical qubits using [[7,1,3]] Steane code on Quantinuum H2 and Helios trapped-ion processors; near-break-even performance on complex error-corrected algorithmic circuits.

idea_genome.problem_genome: Willow demonstrates below-threshold QEC memory, but running actual quantum algorithms on logical qubits — with logical gate operations including T gates and multi-qubit operations — has not been demonstrated.
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: [[7,1,3]] Steane error correction code on trapped-ion hardware (Quantinuum H2 and Helios); fault-tolerant QAOA and HHL implementation with error-corrected logical gates; repeated QEC cycles with enhanced error correction improving algorithm performance.
idea_genome.observation_genome: Fault-tolerant QAOA shows improved performance with increased layers and T gates on 5-6 logical qubits; circuits up to 12 logical qubits show better-than-random performance; enhanced error correction cycles demonstrably boost algorithm results.
idea_genome.limitation_genome: Demonstrated on small problem instances where classical computers still solve faster; fault-tolerant overhead means quantum advantage requires much larger logical qubit counts and below-threshold error rates at scale not yet achieved.

edges.evolution_focus: Surface code theory radiates from abstract blueprint to physical implementation on superconducting transmon hardware; theory prescriptions instantiated in experimental platform.
Scales from 2-qubit threshold demonstration to 21-qubit cyclic repetition code; adds repeated syndrome extraction cycles and correlated decoder; same superconducting platform.
Extends 1D repetition code to 2D surface code on 72-qubit chip; adds second error type (X and Z); same Sycamore architecture, larger code distances.
Willow demonstrates clear below-threshold exponential suppression — the phase transition from above-threshold to below-threshold marks a paradigm shift in fault-tolerant quantum computing.

Fault-tolerant QAOA/HHL execution crosses the threshold from demonstrating QEC memory to actually running fault-tolerant algorithms on logical qubits — the qualitative shift from storing quantum states to computing with them.

open_question: Fault-tolerant QAOA and HHL are demonstrated but on small problem instances where classical computers still win. What mechanism would enable fault-tolerant quantum algorithms at the scale where quantum advantage is achieved for practically relevant problem sizes — requiring both below-threshold logical gates and efficient magic state factories at scale?

question.name: Question
question.description: Present only the domain/subfield and the curated May-2026 research question; ask the model to propose a next-step research direction in Quantum Computing / Superconducting Qubits.

library.name: Library
library.description: Present the curated research frontier plus 6 trace papers in random order with paper titles and brief abstracts, but without explicit lineage order, genome fields, or dynamics labels; ask the model to propose the next research direction.

lineage.name: Lineage
lineage.description: Present the curated research frontier plus the ordered trace with genome metadata, GenomeDiff-style change evidence, and parent-child relationships; ask the model to predict the next evolutionary step without naming predefined category labels.

frontier_evidence.title: Fault-Tolerant Execution of Error-Corrected Quantum Algorithms
frontier_evidence.unresolved_gap: Demonstrated on small problem instances where classical computers still solve faster; fault-tolerant overhead means quantum advantage requires much larger logical qubit counts and below-threshold error rates at scale not yet achieved.
frontier_evidence.why_frontier: Used as the May 2026 frontier anchor: the arena question targets a limitation that remains unresolved at the endpoint of this lineage.

frontier_status.manual_review: Re-audited on 2026-05-05 for appendix release: active arena trace has a 2025/2026 frontier endpoint, a non-solved open research question, valid forward lineage edges, and generation settings aligned with post-hoc dynamics evaluation.
frontier_status.prompt_role: The same research question is shown in Question, Library, and Lineage settings; only the supporting literature/lineage context changes.
